# Gentle Care Nursing Services

Gentle Care Nursing Services is a Sydney-based provider of in-home nursing and personal care. We are a registered NDIS provider and a DVA Contracted Community Nursing Provider, supporting eligible Veteran Card holders with clinically required nursing and personal care services at home.

## What we do

- In-home nursing and personal care across Sydney and surrounds
- NDIS-funded support for participants, families, and support coordinators
- DVA Community Nursing for eligible Veteran Card holders
- Aged care at home through Support at Home, CHSP, and DVA
- Private nursing and personal care

## Services in detail

- Post-hospital care: Skilled nursing and personal care during the transition home after a hospital stay, with visits coordinated from discharge and stepped down as recovery progresses.
- Complex care: Specialised in-home nursing for people with higher clinical needs such as tracheostomy care, PEG feeding, catheter management, and complex wound care.
- Hospital at Home: Hospital-level nursing and personal care delivered in the home for people who are clinically stable but still need close nursing oversight after an acute admission.
- Palliative care: Comfort-focused nursing and personal care at home for people with life-limiting illness, supporting symptom management, dignity, and family wellbeing alongside the palliative care team.
- Personal care: Respectful, consistent assistance with bathing, dressing, grooming, and mobility to help people maintain independence and dignity at home.
- Nursing care: Professional clinical support from AHPRA-registered nurses covering wound management, medication administration, health monitoring, and chronic disease support at home.
- Overnight support: Active overnight nursing or sleepover support depending on care needs, funding and staff availability.
- Community participation: Support for NDIS participants and older Australians to engage in social activities, attend appointments, and build connections in the community.
- Assistance with daily living: Practical help with meal preparation, household tasks, laundry, and daily routines to support independent living at home.

## Who we serve

- NDIS participants — /ndis
- DVA Veteran Card holders — /dva
- Older Australians on Support at Home or CHSP — /aged-care
- Private clients — /private-nursing
- Referrers: hospitals, GPs, support coordinators — /referrers

- Inner West — /areas/inner-west
- Sydney CBD & East — /areas/sydney-cbd-east
- North Shore — /areas/north-shore
- Western Sydney — /areas/western-sydney
- South Sydney — /areas/south-sydney
- Northern Beaches — /areas/northern-beaches

## Key facts

- Registered NDIS provider and DVA Contracted Community Nursing Provider
- We aim to respond to all enquiries within 24 hours, with urgent referrals prioritised
- Led by a care professional with over 10 years of hands-on experience in disability, aged care and in-home support

## Common questions

**Q: Are you registered with NDIS and DVA?**
A: Yes. Gentle Care Nursing is fully registered with the NDIS and is a DVA Contracted Community Nursing Provider, supporting eligible Veteran Card holders with clinically required nursing and personal care services at home. We meet all required quality and safety standards.

**Q: How do I get started?**
A: Submit an enquiry through our Request Care form, or contact us by phone or email. We aim to respond within 24 hours with clear next steps, and urgent referrals are prioritised.

**Q: What funding do you accept?**
A: We accept NDIS, DVA, aged care (Support at Home and CHSP), and private funding. Our team can help you understand which options apply to your situation.

**Q: Is DVA care free for veterans?**
A: For eligible veterans with a DVA Gold or White Card, services are provided at no out-of-pocket cost. We handle all claims directly with DVA on your behalf.

**Q: What makes Gentle Care different?**
A: We take on fewer clients so each person receives more dedicated, attentive care — personalised care plans, consistent carers, prompt responses, and genuine support for families and referral partners.
